hook.script: Did you know that at the quantum level, things can be in multiple places at once? It's like a plant being both watered and not watered until you observe it. Quantum mechanics introduces a world of probabilities and uncertainties.
visualCues: Close-up of a seedling in a greenhouse, alternating between being watered and dry

context.script: Quantum mechanics isn't just abstract theory; it's the foundation of technologies like lasers and semiconductors. Understanding it is key to unlocking the future of computing and materials science.
visualCues: Animated diagram showing the progression from quantum principles to a laser beam being used in surgery

segments.title: Superposition & Entanglement
segments.script: Quantum mechanics introduces superposition and entanglement. Imagine a compost heap where a leaf is simultaneously decomposing and not decomposing. That's superposition. Now picture two leaves entangled; changing one instantly affects the other, no matter how far apart.
visualCues: Split screen showing a leaf in a compost heap, one side decomposing, the other fresh. Morph to two entangled leaves connected by a glowing line.

recap.script: So, quantum mechanics deals with uncertainty and probabilities at the smallest scales. It underpins many modern technologies and opens doors to quantum computing. Keep exploring this fascinating field!

blocks.title: Introduction to Quantum Mechanics
blocks.heroPrompt: A complex network of interconnected nodes, glowing with energy, representing quantum entanglement and superposition.
blocks.description: This lesson introduces the fundamental principles of quantum mechanics, explaining its counterintuitive concepts and implications for future technologies.
objectives: Define quantum mechanics in plain terms
Explain the concepts of superposition and entanglement
Describe the probabilistic nature of quantum mechanics
Recognise the potential applications of quantum mechanics in computation

blocks.quote: Quantum mechanics: where things can be in multiple places at once, until you look.

blocks.label: Learning Objective
blocks.text: After this lesson, you will be able to explain the core principles of **quantum mechanics**, including **superposition** and **entanglement**, and their potential impact on future technologies.

blocks.title: Visual Insight: Quantum Superposition
blocks.videoPrompt: SCENE: Time-lapse of a seedling sprouting. MOTION: Slow orbital push-in. LIGHTING: Soft morning light. PEDAGOGICAL LINK: Shows the potential of multiple states existing simultaneously.
blocks.caption: Quantum superposition allows particles to exist in multiple states at once, much like a seedling exploring various growth paths.

cards.imagePrompt: A microscopic view of atoms arranged in a crystal lattice.
cards.title: Quantum Realm
cards.description: Deals with the behaviour of matter and energy at the atomic and subatomic levels, where classical physics breaks down.

cards.imagePrompt: A probability wave representing the likelihood of finding a particle in a specific location.
cards.title: Probabilistic Nature
cards.description: Outcomes are not deterministic but are described by probabilities, reflecting the inherent uncertainty in quantum systems.

cards.imagePrompt: Two entangled particles, represented by interconnected nodes, reacting simultaneously.
cards.title: Quantum Entanglement
cards.description: Two or more particles become linked, and the state of one instantaneously influences the state of the others, regardless of distance.

insights.title: Reality Isn't Always Real
insights.body: Quantum mechanics suggests that what we perceive as 'real' is just one possibility among many, dictated by observation.

insights.title: Intuition Fails
insights.body: Our everyday experiences don't prepare us for the quantum world. Intuition based on classical physics can be misleading.

insights.title: Future is Quantum
insights.body: Quantum mechanics is not just theoretical; it's the foundation for technologies that will revolutionise computing and materials science.

blocks.heading: The Quantum World
paragraphs: Quantum mechanics is the branch of physics that deals with the very small: atoms and subatomic particles. Unlike classical physics, which accurately describes the world we see around us, quantum mechanics reveals a world of uncertainty and probability.

At the heart of quantum mechanics are concepts like *superposition* and *entanglement*. Superposition means a quantum system can exist in multiple states at the same time. Imagine a seed in a greenhouse that could be both germinating and dormant until observed. Entanglement, on the other hand, links two or more particles together in such a way that they share the same fate, no matter how far apart they are. It’s like two grafted plants where changes in one instantly affect the other.

The key mental model for understanding quantum mechanics is the idea of **probability waves**. Instead of definite positions and velocities, particles are described by probability waves, which indicate the likelihood of finding them in a particular state or location. When we measure a quantum system, we 'collapse' the wave function, forcing the system into one definite state.

cards.imagePrompt: A depiction of a quantum bit (qubit) existing in a superposition of 0 and 1.
cards.title: Qubits
cards.description: The basic unit of quantum information, existing in a superposition of states, unlike classical bits that are either 0 or 1.

cards.imagePrompt: A schematic diagram of quantum entanglement linking two qubits.
cards.title: Entanglement
cards.description: A phenomenon where two or more qubits become linked, and the state of one instantaneously influences the state of the others.

cards.imagePrompt: An illustration of quantum gates performing operations on qubits.
cards.title: Quantum Gates
cards.description: The building blocks of quantum circuits, performing operations on qubits analogous to logic gates in classical computing.

steps.label: Initialisation
steps.description: Set qubits to a known state

steps.label: Superposition
steps.description: Place qubits in a superposition of states

steps.label: Computation
steps.description: Apply quantum gates to perform calculations

steps.label: Measurement
steps.description: Measure the final state of the qubits

blocks.description: This diagram shows how two entangled particles can affect each other instantaneously, regardless of distance.

blocks.question: What happens to an entangled particle when you measure the state of its entangled pair?
options: It instantaneously assumes a correlated state.
It collapses into a random state.
blocks.reveal: When you measure one entangled particle, the other instantaneously assumes a correlated state, regardless of the distance between them. This is a key feature of quantum entanglement.

blocks.scenario: A research team wants to develop a new drug using quantum simulations.
blocks.challenge: Simulating molecular interactions accurately is computationally expensive using classical computers.
blocks.resolution: They use a quantum computer to perform the simulations, leveraging quantum superposition and entanglement to explore a vast range of molecular configurations efficiently.

blocks.heading: Quantum Mechanics in Action
blocks.intro: Quantum mechanics is not just a theoretical concept; it's revolutionising various industries.

tabs.label: Healthcare
tabs.imagePrompt: A quantum computer simulating a drug molecule.
tabs.scenarioBody: Quantum computers can simulate molecular interactions to identify promising drug candidates, accelerating the drug discovery process and reducing development costs. <mark>This allows for the creation of more effective and personalised medicine.</mark>

tabs.label: Finance
tabs.imagePrompt: A quantum algorithm optimising a financial portfolio.
tabs.scenarioBody: Quantum algorithms can optimise financial portfolios by analysing vast amounts of data and identifying optimal investment strategies. <mark>This leads to higher returns and reduced risk.</mark>

tabs.label: Materials Science
tabs.imagePrompt: A quantum simulation of a new material with enhanced properties.
tabs.scenarioBody: Quantum simulations can design new materials with enhanced properties, such as superconductivity and high strength. <mark>This enables the creation of advanced technologies and products.</mark>

tabs.label: Cybersecurity
tabs.imagePrompt: A quantum key distribution system securing communication.
tabs.scenarioBody: Quantum key distribution (QKD) provides secure communication channels by leveraging the principles of quantum mechanics. <mark>This ensures that sensitive information remains protected from eavesdropping.</mark>

blocks.title: Quantum Circuit Simulation
blocks.intro: Simulate a simple quantum circuit and observe the outcome.
blocks.description: This simulation demonstrates how quantum gates operate on qubits. The initial state of the qubits is set to |0⟩. Applying a Hadamard gate puts the first qubit into a superposition. Measuring the qubits collapses their state to either |0⟩ or |1⟩.
blocks.codeSnippet:
```python
from qiskit import QuantumCircuit, Aer, execute

qc = QuantumCircuit(2, 2)
qc.h(0)
qc.cx(0, 1)
qc.measure([0, 1], [0, 1])

backend = Aer.get_backend('qasm_simulator')
job = execute(qc, backend, shots=1024)
result = job.result()
counts = result.get_counts(qc)
print(counts)
```

blocks.title: Quantum Decoherence
blocks.text: Quantum systems are extremely sensitive to their environment. Interactions with the environment can cause decoherence, destroying the quantum states and leading to errors in computation.

blocks.instruction: Improve the following description of quantum entanglement:
blocks.weakPrompt: Entanglement is when two particles are linked together somehow.
blocks.modelAnswer: Quantum entanglement is a phenomenon where two or more particles become linked in such a way that they share the same fate, no matter how far apart they are. Measuring the state of one particle instantaneously influences the state of the other, even if they are light-years away. For example, if two entangled photons are created with opposite polarisations, measuring the polarisation of one will instantly determine the polarisation of the other.

blocks.heading: Reflection
paragraphs: How might our understanding of the universe change if quantum mechanics becomes the dominant framework for all physical laws?

blocks.title: If you remember only three things...
points: Quantum mechanics governs the behaviour of matter at the atomic and subatomic levels.
Superposition allows quantum systems to exist in multiple states simultaneously.
Entanglement links two or more particles, enabling instantaneous correlations.

imageContext.imagePrompt: A qubit in superposition, represented by a Bloch sphere.

questions.questionText: What is superposition in quantum mechanics?

options.text: A particle existing in a single, definite state.
A particle existing in multiple states simultaneously.
A particle not existing at all.

questions.correctFeedback: Correct! Superposition allows particles to be in multiple states at the same time.
questions.incorrectFeedback: Incorrect. Superposition is the ability of a quantum system to be in multiple states at once.

questions.questionText: True or false: Measuring one entangled particle instantaneously affects the state of the other, regardless of distance.
questions.correctFeedback: Correct! Entanglement creates an instantaneous correlation between particles.
questions.incorrectFeedback: Incorrect. Entanglement allows for instantaneous correlation between particles, regardless of distance.

questions.questionText: Quantum computers use ______ to perform calculations.
questions.correctFeedback: Correct! Quantum computers use qubits to perform calculations.
questions.incorrectFeedback: Incorrect. Qubits are the basic unit of information in quantum computing.

terms.definition: The branch of physics dealing with the behaviour of matter and energy at the atomic and subatomic levels.
The ability of a quantum system to exist in multiple states simultaneously.
A phenomenon where two or more particles become linked, sharing the same fate regardless of distance.
The basic unit of quantum information, existing in a superposition of states.
The loss of quantum coherence due to interactions with the environment.

keyTakeaway: Quantum mechanics governs the behaviour of matter at the atomic and subatomic levels, introducing concepts like superposition and entanglement.
discussionPrompt: How might quantum computing change the world in the next 20 years?
nextTopicTeaser: Next, we'll explore the basics of machine learning.
